body_txt: Food, glorious food. Considering that sustenance is a basic need to live, and all the processes needed to bring food to plates, it's no wonder that it's often regarded so highly and even reverently. So, what happens when food is left to rot, thrown away, or otherwise wasted? A special kind of Berserk Button : wasting food. If a character sees another throwing away perfectly good food, or "disrespecting it" in some way (usually smacking it from someone and letting it fall to the ground), they snap. The notion of taking food for granted can be borderline incomprehensible to them, and the one responsible will be in for an earful. They won't take excuses, not even if someone Does Not Like Spam or, in more extreme cases, if they're allergic . Sometimes, they'll even explain to the waster why they're so angry, or be desperate enough to fetch an edible from its disposal bin , no matter if Even the Rats Won't Touch It . Depictions can range from Comical Overreacting , Serious Business (if the reaction is shared by a majority), or anywhere in-between. Usually, this comes about in a character because they were very hungry in the past , and they now know how important food truly is. Or maybe their own parents had this button and it was passed down to them. A common sentiment or argument against wasting food is to Appeal to Worse Problems by stating that people are starving elsewhere. Truth in Television : Some cultures consider it rude to leave food on your plate. For example, a fundamental concept in Japanese culture, mottainai , is regret over wasting something, which is one reason why their food portions tend to be small (so as not to risk wastage). On the flip side, this sort of behavior can also lead to eating disorders, where people become overweight specifically because they were taught to clean their plate no matter what. Compare Lost Food Grievance , which is an instance where someone mourns the loss of (usually) their own food, which can happen without someone wasting it. If the work has an aesop against wasting food, that falls under Wicked Wastefulness . A character with this trait would be particularly offended by someone tossing food just to spite its cook , regardless of if they're the target or not.
